note: RECOVERED from po/SESSION_FINDINGS_20260707.md (2026-07-12). This definition was LOST when it lived only in SPEC_BACKLOG.md. It is Erik's, given explicitly, validated in live play, and it is CANON. Design canon lives in content packs — never only in the backlog.

theDefinition.whatAGambitIs: A set of CONNECTED CHALLENGES FORMING A WHOLE, each solvable MULTIPLE WAYS, PLANNED ACROSS BEFORE RUNNING — not resolved turn-by-turn.
theDefinition.theElementsDefineIt: ⛔ THE NECESSARY ELEMENTS DEFINE A GAMBIT — NOT THE STEP COUNT. A three-step plan is not 'less of a gambit' than a six-step one.
theDefinition.fewerStepsAreHARDER: ⛔ COUNTER-INTUITIVE AND LOAD-BEARING: fewer steps are HARDER, because there is less SLACK. Difficulty ≈ the INVERSE of step-count slack.
theDefinition.theGambitGROWS: ⛔ INVOKING A FALLBACK ADDS STEPS. The gambit GROWS mid-run as fallbacks fire. A single-path plan dies on one bad roll; a gambit ABSORBS it.
theDefinition.validatedLive: Commission House: the primary line for a step FAILED (rolled 51) and the gambit survived on a stacked, character-fit fallback (rolled 79). That is the whole mechanic, demonstrated.

gambitApt.note: ⚠️ THIS SUPERSEDES the heuristics in SNG-043 and refines SNG-077. Aevi twice guessed at this instead of using Erik's definition, which already existed.
gambitApt.condition: A scene is GAMBIT-APT when it presents CONNECTED CHALLENGES — plural, related, forming a whole — EACH admitting MULTIPLE SOLUTION PATHS, such that PLANNING ACROSS THEM beats reacting to them one at a time.
notGambitApt: A careful or planned approach to a SINGLE obstacle.
A scene with several open conversational threads (that is texture, not structure).
Anything where sequencing does not change the outcome.

threeProvenShapes.name: Social / political — The Unwinnable Duel at the Redline
patterns: HONEST MISDIRECTION — every layer TRUE; the power came from what the opponent MISUNDERSTOOD, not from what the player faked.
OWN-MECHANIC REVERSAL — the villain destroyed by the honor-forms he had weaponized.
CHARACTER-NATIVE VICTORY — the Ent won as an organizer and grove-being, not as a generic warrior.

threeProvenShapes.name: Party puzzle — The Silent Door
patterns: Solved by UNDERSTANDING, not force. The antagonist was a thousand-year MISUNDERSTANDING, not an enemy.
QUESTION THE CLOCK — the player asked why a countdown mattered if the thing was locked in a vault. The pressure was genuinely benign, and rewarding that INVERTED the scene.
COST-AS-RESPONSIBILITY — the winning toll was an ongoing caretaking promise, not a one-time payment.
threeProvenShapes.requires: ⛔ TRUE SIMULTANEOUS PARTY TURNS (SNG-038). The GM twice collapsed a called-for simultaneous 3-channel read into round-robin. THE PUZZLE CANNOT BE EXPRESSED ROUND-ROBIN. This makes SNG-038 a REQUIREMENT for co-op puzzles, not a nice-to-have.

threeProvenShapes.name: Logistics vs a system — The Commission House
patterns: NO VILLAIN. The antagonist was a ward + a sweep + questioning + a clock.
Won on MANEUVER and REFRAME-THE-TOOL: stop folding OUT through the ward; fold SMALL AND SIDEWAYS into an already-cleared room. Repositioning, not escape.

gmRules.holdThePlanLevel: ⛔ RECURRING GM FAILURE MODE (Erik flagged it directly, TWICE): puzzles FEEL stepwise, so the GM collapses 'declare plan → resolve with maneuvers inside' into turn-by-turn 'now what?' prompting. **GM RULE: HOLD THE PLAN-LEVEL VIEW AGAINST THE PUZZLE'S STEPWISE PULL.** A gambit is declared as a whole and resolved as a whole; the maneuvers live INSIDE it.
gmRules.engineOwed: `gambit.completionBonusXp` (=10) is in resolution.json and the engine must READ it, not hardcode a literal.
